description.problem: People often struggle to coordinate events, communicate and stay updated within their social circles in one unified place.
description.solution: Built a prototype that combines messaging and calendar‑based group coordination. The backend uses a PHP API with a JSON data layer, while the frontend is implemented in vanilla JavaScript with custom components, view‑specific modules, a lightweight routing approach and a pub/sub‑driven state system. The project focuses on architecture, modularity and team‑based full‑stack development rather than a finished product.

description.problem: Teams needed a secure and structured way to manage inventory with proper authentication, role control and reliable data handling.
description.solution: Built a full‑stack inventory system with JWT auth, RBAC, modular Deno backend, structured logging and a React frontend. Focus on clarity, maintainability and DevOps‑oriented architecture.

description.problem: Travelers needed a way to create, save and manage packing lists across multiple trips with persistent user data.
description.solution: Built an agile full‑stack service with authentication via cookies, internal/external API integration, automated tests and SPA navigation. Contributed to both backend structure and frontend architecture.

description.problem: Users needed a simple interface to manage, search and validate city data with real‑time feedback.
description.solution: Created a CRUD web service with dynamic UI updates, search functionality, in‑browser API testing, consistent error handling and a modular codebase separating frontend logic, tests and server API.
